adds.rationale: Rocket Lab signed five Neutron contracts plus a $190M / 20-launch HASTE order in Q1 2025 and is tracking a Neutron debut in 2H 2025 - the pure-play rockets/spacecraft exposure the profile names as a 'next wave' and which the watchlist has had zero coverage of since ARKX was removed last quarter.

news_evidence.summary: Rocket Lab Q1 2025 update: five Neutron contracts signed and a $190M, 20-launch HASTE order, lifting backlog 20% quarter-on-quarter; Neutron debut on track for second half of 2025.
Rocket Lab signed a second multi-launch Electron contract with Japan-based iQPS in Q1 2025, one of the largest Electron launch agreements to date.

removes.rationale: BOTZ is a diluted robotics ETF (top holdings span industrial automation conglomerates and Japanese factory-automation names) whose constituents do not really map to the rapid-iteration robotics wave the profile is targeting; ISRG remains as the pure-play robotics exposure, and dropping BOTZ frees a cap slot for a higher-conviction rockets pure-play (RKLB) - same logic as the ARKX-for-LNG swap last quarter.

news_evidence.summary: Robotics ETFs like BOTZ underperformed pure-play robotics and AI-infrastructure names through Q1 2025 as investors rotated toward concentrated bets following the DeepSeek-driven AI repricing on Jan 27.

rationale_overall: One swap at the cap: add RKLB to plug the rockets/spacecraft bucket the profile names as a 'next wave' but the watchlist has had zero coverage of since the ARKX trim last quarter (Q1 2025 brought five new Neutron contracts and a $190M HASTE order, with a Neutron debut due 2H 2025), and remove BOTZ since it is a diluted robotics ETF whose constituents do not map cleanly to the user's robotics thesis (ISRG remains as the pure-play). Net effect: the watchlist trades a soft thematic ETF for a concrete pure-play rockets name, while still holding AI (MSFT, NVDA), robotics (ISRG), aging-demographics (LLY), quantum (QTUM, intact despite Huang's Jan 8 '15-30 years' comments since the profile explicitly names quantum as a long-horizon next wave), nuclear (NLR), and geopolitical-energy (LNG).
